nameMeanings.name: Stahl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a steelsmith, an armorer or a steel merchant, from Middle High German stāl and German Stahl ‘steel, armor’. The Jewish surname is often artificial rather than occupational. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Denmark, and some other European countries.
Swedish (Ståhl): soldier's name from ståhl, an ornamental spelling of stål ‘steel’. Compare Steel and Steele .

variants.name: Stahlman
meanings: Americanized form of German Stahlmann: variant of Stahl with the addition of Middle High German man ‘man’. Compare Stallman .
Swedish (Ståhlman): soldier's name, a compound of an ornamental spelling of stål ‘steel’ (see Stahl 2) + man ‘man’. Compare Steelman .

variants.name: Stehling
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hermann, Kurt.
German: variant of Stähling, a patronymic from Stahl .

variants.name: Stahle
meanings: German (Stähle): variant of Stahl 1.
Swedish (Ståhle): variant of Ståhl (see Stahl 2).

variants.name: Stahli
meanings: Swiss German and South German (Stähli): occupational name for a smith or an armorer, from a diminutive of Stahl ‘steel, armor’.

variants.name: Stehle
meanings: German:
variant of Stähle (see Stahl ).
habitational name from any of the places called Steele, Stehle, Stehla, or Stehlen.

variants.name: Stehlin
meanings: German (Baden, Alsace): from an Alemannic diminutive of Middle High German stāl ‘steel’ (see Stahl ).

variants.name: Steel
meanings: English: variant of   Steele  .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German Stahl and Swedish Ståhl or Stål, all meaning ‘steel’.

variants.name: Staal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Ursel.
Dutch: metonymic occupational name for a steelsmith, an armorer or a steel merchant, from Middle Dutch stael ‘steel’.
Dutch (Van Staal, Van der Staal): habitational name for someone from any of various places called De Staal, for example, in Izenberge, West Flanders.

variants.name: Stehl
meanings: German: variant of Stähle (see Stahl ).

nameMeanings.name: Rosina
meanings: A diminutive of the name Rose, or of the Italian form Rosa

en.lifeSummary: Rosina Stahl was born in 1596, in Bad Herrenalb, Calw, Baden-Württemberg, Germany. She had at least 1 son. She died in 1660, in Germany, at the age of 64.
